mapping_metadata.analyzer: Senior Test Automation Mapping Specialist Agent

test_case_mappings.test_case_description: Test case to verify the Billing Cycle Period card behavior on Print and Payment History page when a plan is paused, including verification of complimentary pages, additional pages, progress bars, tooltips, and page count displays.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Enroll the Ink printer with 50 pages plan or higher.
precondition_mapping.notes: This batch contains helper files but no printer enrollment methods. Printer enrollment is likely handled by enrollment_helper.py or oss_emulator_helper.py methods, but the specific enrollment with plan selection is not in this batch. The OssEmulatorHelper.setup_oss_emulator() method exists but requires a printer object to be passed in, suggesting enrollment happens elsewhere.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the Subscription has no free months info.
precondition_mapping.notes: No API helpers or verification methods found in this batch for checking subscription free months status. This would require API calls to subscription service which are not present in these helper files.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the subscription is "subscribed" status.
precondition_mapping.notes: No subscription status verification methods found in this batch. This would require API calls or UI verification methods not present in these helper files.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Click the Pause Plan button on CIT modal or "Pause Instant Ink" link to trigger the pause plan.
precondition_mapping.notes: No pause plan methods found in this batch. The helpers in this batch focus on authentication, landing page, OSS emulator, overview, and print history operations, but do not contain pause plan functionality.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Verifies that the billing table displays the correct total pages printed value. Retrieves the inner text of the total_pages_printed element, extracts numeric values, and asserts the first number equals the expected total_pages_printed parameter.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Step 14 expected result requires verifying total pages printed displays 15 pages. This method directly verifies the total pages printed value in the billing table, which is part of the Billing Cycle Period card.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Verifies that selecting a previous billing cycle period changes the display from 'Current' to a past period. Clicks the billing cycle dropdown, selects the first previous period option, and verifies the title no longer contains 'Current'.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: While not directly used in this test case, this method demonstrates billing cycle selection functionality which is related to the Billing Cycle Period card being tested. The test case focuses on the current billing cycle, but this method shows how to interact with billing cycle selection.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Selects a specific billing cycle period from the dropdown by index. Opens the billing cycle dropdown, selects the period based on index (1, 2, or 3), and verifies the table updates by checking print_history_table_button visibility.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Provides billing cycle selection functionality which is related to the Billing Cycle Period card. While the test case doesn't explicitly select different billing cycles, this method shows how to interact with the billing cycle dropdown that is part of the card being tested.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Verifies multiple fields in the billing section against expected values provided in a dictionary. Can verify total_pages_printed, pages, rollover, additional, plan_price, overage_price, previous, charges, tax, and total fields. Uses currency validation and number extraction for comprehensive billing data verification.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This comprehensive method can verify multiple aspects of the Billing Cycle Period card including total pages printed (step 14), complimentary pages value (step 8), and additional pages value (step 12). It provides a single method to validate the complete billing section data that the test case is verifying across multiple steps.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Normalizes currency values to a consistent format for comparison. Handles various currency formats, symbols (dollar, euro, pound), removes spaces and commas, converts to float with 2 decimal places. Returns '0.00' for empty or N/A values.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Utility method for normalizing currency values which may be useful if the test case needs to verify any pricing information on the Billing Cycle Period card. While the current test case focuses on page counts and progress bars, this utility could be useful for extended billing verification.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Validates that a currency field contains the expected value with flexible matching options. Retrieves text from selector, normalizes both expected and actual values, supports exact or partial matching. Handles integer values as cents (divides by 100).
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Provides currency field validation which could be useful for verifying any pricing information on the Billing Cycle Period card. While the test case focuses on page counts, this method demonstrates the pattern for field validation that could be adapted for other card elements.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Verifies that a specific notification text is present in the print history table. Expands the table if hidden, waits for content to load, retrieves table inner text, and asserts the notification text is present.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Step 5 expected result requires verifying plan pause information is displayed. This method can verify notification text in the print history table, which could include plan pause notifications. While it checks the table rather than the card specifically, it demonstrates the pattern for verifying text content on the print history page.

matched_methods.method_signature:
@staticmethod
def verify_plan_info(page, plan_value: str, plan_pages: str)
matched_methods.what_it_does: Verifies that the plan information displays the correct plan value and plan pages. Waits for print_history_card visibility, retrieves plan_price_text content, extracts numbers, and asserts first number equals plan_value and second equals plan_pages.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Verifies plan information on the print history page which is related to the Billing Cycle Period card context. While not directly verifying the specific card elements in the test case, it shows how to verify plan-related information on the same page.

matched_helpers.what_it_does: Provides comprehensive static utility methods for testing and validating the print history and payment history page. Contains methods for selecting billing cycle periods, verifying billing table data, normalizing currency values, verifying billing sections, validating FAQs, downloading invoices, and verifying notifications.
matched_helpers.relevance_reason: This helper class is directly relevant to the test case as it provides methods for interacting with and verifying the Print and Payment History page where the Billing Cycle Period card is located. Multiple methods can be used for verifying billing data, page counts, and card content.

test_case_mappings.batch_coverage_summary: This batch contains helper files focused on authentication (HPIDHelper), landing page operations (LandingPageHelper), OSS emulator setup (OssEmulatorHelper), overview page operations (OverviewHelper), and print history operations (PrintHistoryHelper). The PrintHistoryHelper is the most relevant to this test case as it provides methods for verifying billing data and page counts on the Print and Payment History page. However, the test case requires very specific UI element interactions (progress bars, tooltips, info icons, pause plan information display) that are not covered by the helper methods in this batch. The helpers provide high-level verification of billing data and page counts but do not provide methods for: 1) Locating and verifying the Billing Cycle Period card container, 2) Verifying progress bar visual appearance (color, fill state), 3) Interacting with info icons and verifying tooltips, 4) Verifying plan pause information display on the card, 5) Simulating page printing via API, 6) Navigating to Print and Payment History page, 7) Refreshing the page to see updated data. The batch provides partial coverage for numeric value verification (total pages, complimentary pages, additional pages) but lacks the UI element locators, visual verification methods, and interaction methods needed for complete test automation. The missing components are likely in page object files (PrintHistoryPage, DashboardSideMenuPage) and API helper files not included in this batch.
